stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Etel Adnan is a multidisciplinary artist.
  • In 1959, her first solo art exhibition, Visions of Beirut, was shown at the Sursock Museum in Beirut, Lebanon.
  • Her poetry collection The Arab Apocalypse was published in 1989 by Post-Apollo Press.
  • Her poetry collection Sea and Fog was published in 2012 by Nightboat Books.
  • She lived part time in Lebanon, where she was born, and part time in Paris and California.
stem: The student wants to introduce the artist’s 1989 poetry collection. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Before she published the books The Arab Apocalypse (1989) and Sea and Fog (2012), Etel Adnan exhibited visual art at the Sursock Museum in Beirut, Lebanon.
Etel Adnan is a true multidisciplinary artist whose works include numerous poetry collections and visual art exhibitions.
In 1959, Etel Adnan exhibited her first solo art exhibition, Visions of Beirut, in Lebanon, her country of birth.
Published in 1989 by Post-Apollo Press, The Arab Apocalypse is a collection of poetry by the multidisciplinary artist Etel Adnan.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. It introduces the 1989 poetry collection by naming the title, publisher, year, and the author’s multidisciplinary identity.

Choice A focuses on visual art rather than introducing the 1989 collection. Choice B introduces Adnan as an artist without focusing on the collection. Choice C describes a 1959 exhibition rather than the 1989 collection.
